name: environmental_licensing_backlog_stonehaven_tru_loy
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. A cross-functional incident desk is managing a domain incident with incomplete information and high reputational exposure. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: environmental licensing backlog stonehaven tru loy. The moral tension is explicitly framed as truth versus loyalty: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. A union steward and a patient-family advocate are both on the bridge demanding incompatible assurances. An automated failover or batch commit fires in twenty-two minutes unless a named approver intervenes. Anyone affected by an automated hold or model output must retain a human escalation path that is actually staffed. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Mei Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Mei Ishikawa, the accountable decision-maker on this Environmental Policy bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes loyalty whenever someone invokes truth.

agents.name: Leila Santos
agents.identity: I am Leila Santos, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so loyalty is not sacrificed by default just because truth feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect loyalty.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Mei Ishikawa must decide under truth versus loyalty. What does Mei Ishikawa do?
